hypothesis: If a chemically-informed latent space derived from periodic table representations is used to condition a diffusion model on adsorption energy and adsorbate identity, then it will generate shell-wise surface compositions that more accurately satisfy chemical constraints and match target adsorption energy distributions compared to a chemically-agnostic baseline, because encoding domain-specific elemental relationships into the latent space provides inductive biases that guide the generative model toward physically meaningful and constraint-compliant catalyst compositions.
